text.title: Firewall Management: Production Examples
text.summary: NERO production server UFW rules, multi-domain setup, debugging an exposed Docker port, and emergency lockout recovery via Hetzner Cloud Console.

section.title: NERO Production Rules
section.p: Ubuntu 24.04, Hetzner CX53. Three external ports only: SSH on 2222, HTTP 80, HTTPS 443. All Docker services bind to 127.0.0.1 — no UFW rules needed for them.

example.code:
Status: active Logging: on (low) Default: deny (incoming), allow (outgoing), deny (routed)
To Action From -- ------ ---- 2222/tcp ALLOW IN Anywhere 80/tcp ALLOW IN Anywhere 443/tcp ALLOW IN Anywhere
example.note: No per-service rules. All backend ports (8100, 8101, 5432, 6379) bind to 127.0.0.1 and are accessed via nginx or SSH tunnel.

section.title: Debugging an Exposed Docker Port
section.p: Symptom: RabbitMQ Management UI (15672) was reachable from the internet despite no UFW rule.

example.code: # Before fix — docker-proxy listening on 0.0.0.0 $ sudo ss -tlnp | grep 15672 LISTEN 0 4096 0.0.0.0:15672 0.0.0.0:* users:(("docker-proxy",pid=1234))
# Fix in docker-compose.yml services: rabbitmq: ports: - "127.0.0.1:15672:15672" # was "15672:15672"
# After fix — bound to 127.0.0.1 $ sudo ss -tlnp | grep 15672 LISTEN 0 4096 127.0.0.1:15672 0.0.0.0:* users:(("docker-proxy",pid=5678))

section.title: Multi-Domain Setup
section.p: Same three UFW rules for all domains. nginx server_name routing handles per-domain logic. No per-project UFW additions needed.

example.code: nero.faion.net → CF → nginx:443 → 127.0.0.1:8100/8101 meetingtax.io → CF → nginx:443 → 127.0.0.1:8000/3000 eulaguard.com → CF → nginx:443 → 127.0.0.1:8001

section.title: Emergency Lockout Recovery (Hetzner)
section.p: If SSH is locked out by a UFW mistake, use the Hetzner Cloud Console VNC session.

example.code: # In Hetzner console VNC session sudo ufw disable sudo ufw allow 22022/tcp sudo ufw enable sudo ufw status

see-also.link: UFW status report with Docker exposure check
